Update dependency go to v1.26.1 (#137)

* Update dependency go to v1.26.1

* fix: update Go and golangci-lint versions for Go 1.26 compatibility

golangci-lint 2.4.0 was built with Go 1.25 and refuses to lint a
project targeting Go 1.26.1. Upgrade to golangci-lint 2.10.1 (first
built with Go 1.26 support from v2.9.0) and bump the setup-go version
to 1.26 to match the project toolchain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: suppress false-positive gosec findings in incident.go

The golangci-lint upgrade (needed for Go 1.26 support) bundles a newer
gosec that flags G704 (SSRF) and G706 (log injection) via taint
analysis. Both are false positives: the HTTP URLs are hardcoded
constants pointing to api.incident.io, and the logged values come from
that trusted API response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add cross-platform focus tracking linked to sessions

iOS: FocusInterruptionTracker counts app-leave events and away time
during focus sessions, with live indicator pill and per-session focus
score. macOS: per-session app usage capture via AppActivityTracker
query window, saved as session-linked AppUsageRecords. Both sync
cross-device via CloudKit — iPhone shows Mac app breakdown, Mac shows
iPhone focus scores. Session log enhanced with color-coded focus
percentages and top-app summaries.

commit.message:
Add in-page import/export to Inventory, Deals, Customers, and Employees pages

Creates reusable ImportExportButton component that enables quick data import/export without leaving the current page. Users can now import historical data or export current data directly from any major database page.

Features:
- Modal-based import workflow (4 steps vs 6-step standalone)
- One-click CSV export of current data
- Pre-configured for page's data type (no detection needed)
- Auto-refreshes page after successful import
- Reuses existing import infrastructure (edge functions, validation, learning system)

Pages updated:
- InventoryPage: Import/export inventory vehicles
- DealsPage: Import/export sales deals
- CustomersPage: Import/export customer records
- TeamPage: Import/export employee data (admin only)

User benefits:
- Faster workflow (stay on page, context-aware)
- Quick exports for backups/sharing
- Same AI column mapping and validation
- Full audit trail via import_sessions

Component: ImportExportButton.jsx
- Reusable for any data type
- Modal UI with upload, mapping, validation, import steps
- Export queries dealer_id filtered data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Persist lifecycle PR state across daemon restarts (Forge-eyb) (#19)

* fix: persist lifecycle PR state across daemon restarts

Bead: Forge-eyb

* Fix thread safety, logging, and error handling in PR lifecycle manager

* fix(lifecycle): use composite key for PR states and persist new PRs

- Use 'anvil/number' as key in Manager.states to avoid collisions across repositories
- Automatically insert new PRs into the database in HandleEvent to ensure they have an ID for future updates
- Update GetState, SetBranch, and Remove signatures to include anvil name
- Add unit test for anvil collision scenario

* fix: address review comments for Forge-eyb

- db.go: replace fragile error-string matching in migrate() with a
  PRAGMA table_info structural column-existence check (comment 6)
- db.go: omit ci_passing from InsertPR so the DB default (1=passing)
  always applies, preventing silent insertion of failing PRs when
  callers leave CIPassing at its zero value (comment 1)
- lifecycle.go: distinguish DB error from not-found in GetPRByNumber;
  on error, fall back to in-memory-only tracking instead of inserting
  a potentially duplicate row (comments 2 & 3)
- lifecycle.go: move UpdatePRLifecycle outside m.mu to avoid blocking
  all event handling during SQLite contention (comments 4 & 5)
- lifecycle_test.go: close and reopen the DB handle before creating m2
  to properly simulate a daemon restart (comments 7 & 8)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures for Forge-eyb

Resolve merge conflict in lifecycle.go - keep composite string key for
states map (anvil/number) and maxRebase field from both sides of conflict.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-eyb

* fix: resolve CI failures for Forge-eyb

* fix: address review comments for Forge-eyb

* test: remove misleading CIPassing from InsertPR test struct

InsertPR intentionally omits ci_passing so the DB default applies.
Setting CIPassing on the struct before insert implies it is written,
which it is not. DB default (true) ensures pr2.CIPassing is still true.

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: fabric IPVLAN overlay, single-fabric-per-node, cluster NIC fix

Replace fabric interface rename (ge-0-0-0 → fab0) with IPVLAN L2
overlay. Physical member keeps its ge-X-0-Y name for XDP/TC
attachment; fab0/fab1 are IPVLAN interfaces for IP addressing and
session sync. This avoids link down/up cycling during rename and
preserves PCI identity.

Single fabric per node: fab0 on node0 (ge-0-0-0), fab1 on node1
(ge-7-0-0). Auto-detect picks local fabric only (not both), fixing
blank FabricPeerAddress on node1.

Cluster NIC layout: remove bpfrx-fabric1 network (single fabric
link), map reth1/LAN to ge-X/0/1 (was ge-X/0/2), keeping reth0/WAN
on ge-X/0/3 (SR-IOV VF).

Fix test scripts: grep "Session State: Valid" (not "State:
Established") matching actual CLI output format.

Tested: make test (all pass), make test-failover (14/14 pass,
7.49 Gbps), split-RG fabric redirect verified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🚚 İrsaliye İş Akışı Tamamlandı

## Yeni Özellikler:
- 📤 İrsaliye talebi oluşturma (Depo → İrsaliye İste butonu)
- 📄 PDF yüklendiğinde otomatik stok çıkışı
- ⏳ Bekleyen irsaliyelerin gösterimi (depo sayfasında)
- 🔗 İrsaliye ile stok hareketi ilişkilendirme

## İş Akışı:
1. Depo: Form doldur → "İrsaliye İste" tıkla
2. Sistem: Waybill talebi oluştur (status: pending)
3. Form bilgileri JSON olarak waybill notes'a kaydedilir
4. Muhasebe: PDF yükle (Faturalar ve İrsaliyeler sayfası)
5. Sistem: PDF yüklenir → warehouse_transaction oluşturulur → Stok çıkışı yapılır

## Teknik Detaylar:
- PDF yüklendiğinde notes'dan form verisi parse edilir
- warehouse_transactions entry otomatik oluşturulur
- waybill.inventory_transaction_id ile ilişkilendirilir
- Bekleyen irsaliyeler depo sayfasında sarı kartlarla gösterilir
- Her 5 dakikada otomatik refresh (silent mode)

## UI Özellikleri:
- 🟡 Sarı kartlarla "İrsaliye Bekleniyor" bölümü
- Ürün adı, miktar, hedef bilgisi görünür
- Talep tarihi görünür
- "⏳ Bekliyor" badge'i

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add quantity parsing to recipe scoring

Parses fractions, decimals, and units (cups, tbsp, lbs, fillets, etc.)
from recipe text. Estimates actual calories per ingredient using a
per-category lookup table. Score now reflects real caloric proportions
instead of raw category counts.

Maple-glazed salmon: 1/4 cup syrup (~190 cal) vs 1 lb salmon (~500 cal)
now correctly scores ~7-8 instead of 5.

commit.message:
feat(cache): add TTL-based correlation result cache

Cache correlate_containers results to .cache/correlations/<md5>.json,
keyed by sorted container names + time_window_seconds + tail. Serves
repeat calls instantly within the TTL window (default 30 min, controlled
by CORRELATION_CACHE_TTL_MINUTES; 0 = disabled).

- config.py: new correlation_cache_ttl_minutes setting (default 30)
- tools.py: _correlation_cache_key(), _read_correlation_cache(),
  _write_correlation_cache(); tool_correlate_containers uses cache-first
  pattern and returns correlation_cache_hit: true/false
- tests/test_correlation_cache.py: 14 new unit tests (key stability,
  TTL expiry, TTL=0 disable, miss/hit flow, use_cache=false bypass)
- docs: WIKI_TOOLS, WIKI_ARCHITECTURE, WIKI_OPERATIONS, WIKI_QUALITY updated

fix: remove identity header requirement from platform key decrypt endpoint (#33)

GET /keys/platform/{provider}/decrypt is for platform-level keys that are
global (not tied to any org/user). The endpoint description said "No
orgId/userId needed" but the middleware and schema both required them.

Extracted the handler into a separately-mounted route without identity
headers, keeping only x-caller-* headers for provider requirements tracking.

commit.message:
Fix robots.txt blocking API endpoints from Googlebot (#552)

Allow Googlebot to fetch public API endpoints

robots.txt was blocking /api/ paths, which prevented Google's Web
Rendering Service from making XHR requests needed to render SPA pages.
This caused actor pages (and others) to show TECHNICAL DIFFICULTIES
error state in Google's index instead of actual content.

API endpoints return JSON, not HTML, so they won't appear in search
results. The /admin/ block remains unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve stability: config-based model detection, template caps, multi-format tool parsing, incremental streaming

- Add config.json-based model type detection (text vs VLM) instead of exception string-matching
- Add TemplateCaps to detect tools/thinking support from tokenizer chat templates
- Extract tool parser into shared module with Qwen, Mistral, Llama 3.x, DeepSeek, and bare JSON support
- Implement incremental streaming for Anthropic endpoint (thinking state machine for non-tool responses)
- Add tool description injection into system message when template lacks native tool support
- Add global error handlers returning proper JSON format per API surface (Anthropic/OpenAI/Ollama)
- Extend parameter mapping (repeat_last_n, min_p, frequency/presence_penalty)
- Improve embeddings robustness (static embed_tokens detection, flexible tensor shapes)
- Preserve exception type and traceback in streaming error reporting

commit.message:
Add Kinesis and DynamoDB Streams polling to Lambda ESM engine

The event source mapping engine now polls Kinesis streams and DynamoDB
Streams in addition to SQS queues. Kinesis polling tracks per-shard
position using sequence numbers. DynamoDB Streams polling reads from
the hook-based record store.

Also improved Lambda code loading to prefer code_bytes (pre-decoded)
over code["ZipFile"] for reliability.

commit.message:
Fix live sync between tabs of same user by using per-tab sender ID

Both browsers logged in as the same user shared the same senderId,
causing each tab to ignore the other's broadcast messages. Now uses
a unique per-tab ID so the same user can collaborate across tabs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add billing pipeline: aggregation, Worldlink processing, order DB, and backfill

- aggregate.py: load MASTER FOR BILLING from weekly logs, validate/standardize
  col S (billing month), filter, sort, and group rows into affidavit buckets
- worldlink.py: parse Worldlink Etere CSVs into A-AC billing rows; validates
  duplicate contract numbers and DAL market isolation
- diagnose.py: one-time diagnostic to check key columns for null values
- orders_db.py: SQLite schema (orders, order_monthly, client_flags) with CRUD;
  tracks expected monthly gross per contract/market and client-level flags for
  notarization (drives affidavit template) and EDI delivery
- order_parser.py: parse Sales Confirmation header and compute monthly gross
  breakout per market from Run Sheet col S + col P
- backfill.py: scan M:\Clients for order files modified since 2025-01-01 and
  populate billing.db; supports --dry-run, --since, --clients-dir, --db
- manage_db.py: CLI to view/set client flags (notarized, EDI) and show which
  orders in a given billing month need notarization or EDI upload

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

SEO audit fixes: remove deprecated schema, fix i18n CSS, strip broken hreflang (#52)

4 critical SEO fixes from full audit (score 58→76):

1. CSS: Show Japanese content by default instead of hiding all [data-lang]
   elements. Previously all text was invisible until JS executed, meaning
   crawlers saw blank pages.

2. Remove all hreflang tags from 89 HTML files and sitemap.xml. The ?lang=en
   URLs served identical Japanese HTML (client-side JS switching only), so
   258 hreflang annotations were sending incorrect signals to Google.

3. Remove deprecated HowTo schema from 27 pages. Google removed HowTo rich
   results in September 2023.

4. Remove FAQPage schema from 72 pages. FAQ rich results restricted to
   government/healthcare sites since August 2023.

Co-authored-by: Hajime Ataka <hajimeataka@Hajime-AtakaMacBook-Air.local>

sync 시작 시 base에 있지만 로컬에 없는 파일을 자동으로 deletedPaths에 추가.
대량 삭제는 기존 delete guard가 보호.

refactor: 调整为正确的多插件 marketplace 结构

- 在根目录添加 .claude-plugin/marketplace.json 作为主配置
- 删除各插件目录下的 marketplace.json
- 每个插件只保留 .claude-plugin/plugin.json

结构：
custom-claude-package/
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── marketplace.json    # 主配置，列出所有插件
└── plugins/
    ├── deploy-workflow/
    │   ├── .claude-plugin/
    │   │   └── plugin.json
    │   ├── agents/
    │   ├── commands/
    │   └── skills/
    └── plugin-creator/
        ├── .claude-plugin/
        │   └── plugin.json
        ├── agents/
        ├── commands/
        └── skills/
